content.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=GetFixedTree
WORK=/app/_kill_gft
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK

# Each loop bound, with its 5 relational mutants.
BOUNDS=(144 256 280 288 32)
declare -A LOOP
LOOP[144]='for (i = 0; i < 144; i++)'
LOOP[256]='for (i = 144; i < 256; i++)'
LOOP[280]='for (i = 256; i < 280; i++)'
LOOP[288]='for (i = 280; i < 288; i++)'
LOOP[32]='for (i = 0; i < 32; i++)'

ORIG=(); MUT=()
for B in "${BOUNDS[@]}"; do
  base="${LOOP[$B]}"
  for op in '<=' '>' '>=' '==' '!='; do
    ORIG+=("$base")
    MUT+=("${base/< $B/$op $B}")
  done
done

killed=0
for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"; O="$O" M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Q$ENV{O}\E/$ENV{M}/' mutated.c
  if ! grep -qF "$M" mutated.c; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx ($M)"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "CCFAIL $idx"; cat /tmp/cc.err; continue; fi
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx: $M  [$out]"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="
=== ls /app ===
/app/kill_cts.sh
/app/kill_gft.sh
/app/kill_tohfr.sh

## How to run CBMC directly

Here is the sequence of commands to verify one function named `<FUNCTION_NAME>`.
The function calls two other functions, `<CALLEE1>` and `<CALLEE2>`.
The function is defined in file `<PATH_TO_C_FILE>`.

```sh
FUNCTION=<FUNCTION_NAME> \
goto-cc -o ${FUNCTION}.goto <PATH_TO_C_FILE> --function ${FUNCTION} \
&& goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 ${FUNCTION}.goto ${FUNCTION}.goto \
&& goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract <CALLEE1> --replace-call-with-contract <CALLEE2> --enforce-contract ${FUNCTION} ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto \
&& cbmc checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto --function ${FUNCTION} --depth 200
```

This will produce a log to the standard output.

### Concrete example of how to run CBMC

To verify specifications for function `partition` defined in file `quicksort.c`,
where `partition`'s body calls function `swap`, run:

```sh
FUNCTION=partition \
&& goto-cc -o ${FUNCTION}.goto quicksort.c --function ${FUNCTION} \
&& goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 ${FUNCTION}.goto ${FUNCTION}.goto \
&& goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract swap --enforce-contract ${FUNCTION} ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto \
&& cbmc checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto --function ${FUNCTION} --depth 200
```

cd /tmp && cat > kill_probe.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/tmp/probe2.c
FUNCTION=GetFixedTree
WORK=/tmp/_kill_probe
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK
BOUNDS=(144 256 280 288 32)
declare -A LOOP
LOOP[144]='for (i = 0; i < 144; i++)'
LOOP[256]='for (i = 144; i < 256; i++)'
LOOP[280]='for (i = 256; i < 280; i++)'
LOOP[288]='for (i = 280; i < 288; i++)'
LOOP[32]='for (i = 0; i < 32; i++)'
ORIG=(); MUT=()
for B in "${BOUNDS[@]}"; do
  base="${LOOP[$B]}"
  for op in '<=' '>' '>=' '==' '!='; do
    ORIG+=("$base"); MUT+=("${base/< $B/$op $B}")
  done
done
killed=0
for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"; O="$O" M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Q$ENV{O}\E/$ENV{M}/' mutated.c
  if ! grep -qF "$M" mutated.c; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx ($M)"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "CCFAIL $idx"; continue; fi
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"; else echo "KILLED    $idx: $M"; killed=$((killed+1)); fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="
EOF
chmod +x kill_probe.sh && ./kill_probe.sh

## GetFixedTree — verified, kill score raised from 0/25 → **3/25**

**Verification:** `VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL` (0 of 24844 failed) under the official flow (`--partial-loops --unwind 5 ... --enforce-contract ... --depth 200`).

**What I found:** The prior memory documented 0/25 as "inherent," but that analysis only considered the in-loop index asserts and the final `forall` ensures — both of which sit past the depth-200 frontier and pass vacuously. It missed one lever: **post-loop "loop ran" asserts**.

After each loop I added an assert that the *first* element the loop writes holds its expected value, e.g. `__CPROVER_assert(ll_lengths[0] == 8, ...)`. A zero-iteration bound mutant (`>`, `>=`, `==`) skips the loop, leaving that element at its havoced `object_whole` value, so the assert fails → mutant killed. It still passes on the original (the loop runs ≥1 of its 5 unwound iterations and writes the element).

**Why exactly 3, not more — measured the depth frontier:**
- Prologue (is_fresh + object_whole havoc over the 288- and 32-element arrays): ~80–120 depth
- Each unwound loop: ~+100 depth
- Post-loop-1 assert reachable at depth ∈ (180, 200]; post-loop-2 at (250, 300]; post-loop-3 at (300, 400]; loops 4–5 beyond 400.

So at the fixed `--depth 200` only loop 1's post-assert is reachable, killing its three zero-iteration mutants. The prologue cost is intrinsic to the required full-strength memory contract and can't be shaved without weakening the spec.

**The remaining 22 are genuinely unkillable under `--unwind 5`:**
- 5 `<=` extra-iteration mutants — diverge only at a boundary index beyond the 5-iteration frontier
- 5 `!=` mutants — equivalent
- 12 zero-iteration mutants on loops 2–5 — past the depth-200 frontier (they *do* kill at depth ~300/400+, confirming the asserts are sound)

I added all five post-loop asserts (the loops 2–5 ones are sound and would kill at higher depth/unwind), updated the function's doc comment, and corrected the memory note (it previously said "0/25 inherent, don't chase"). The C logic is unchanged — only specifications/assertions were added.
